channel.title: Networking on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Networking on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: The Importance Of Conversation
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the importance of conversation.

I know to some people I might go. You know my secret weapon: it’s talking. Maybe that’s not so secret, since all I do on this channel is talk and talk and a lot of people I’ve worked with say “Wow, you talk a lot”.

But and also sometimes I worry that I babble like a babbling idiot. But recently I said that to a friend of mine and he said “No, you’re more like a babbling brook.

item.title: Networking With Game Developers
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about networking, something that I have never been good at but that I recommend you do.

First off, I got a dog that’s not feeling good lying on my feet and I just had gum surgery. So you may see some stitches poking out. Um, just something happens when you get older. Um, every it seems like when I go to the doctor, the dentists are always saying: well, Mr Kane, at your age, you need take this, or we need to do this, or we need to put a camera here. I basically, when the doctor before he started the surgery, I asked him if he could give me pouty lips like Renee zel wigger. How did he do anyway, going to talk past the stitches and the dog will, who will occasionally probably be shaking her head?

item.title: PAX 2024
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about pxs 2024.

Yes, I went to P here in Seattle over the Labor Day weekend, super fun, and I want to kind of talk about it now. I’ve been to a half a dozen PA conferences before. I’ve been to this one, which sometimes I think it’s called PA West or PAX Prime. I just call it PA. It’s like what I do with Fallout. The first one is just Fallout.

item.title: Unused GDC 1999 Roadtrip Presentation
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about RPG 2000.

This is a presentation that I was going to give at the 1999 GDC road trip in Austin. Let me give you some context. I was going through my archives and on the inside — the archive of the very first machine I had at troyka — I think I had three different machines — I found a PowerPoint called GDC 99 road trip. I remember making this PowerPoint vaguely, but I did not go to the GDC 99 road trip convention in Austin, which leads me to believe — because, by the way, there were no other notes, no notes on this talk, no notes about the talk, no emails back and forth — that either I made this and never submitted it, or I submitted it or didn’t get in, or maybe even I got in and decided not to go. I have no memory, and what’s weird about this is I would have been making this in early summer of 99, as if I wasn’t busy enough. We were.

item.title: Game Conferences
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game conferences.

Um, the way I’ve got I decided to do this was I’ve had several people ask: you know, oh, are you gonna, are you making a new game, are you gonna Kickstarter or do you gonna make a sequel to one of your games? And I keep saying no. And finally, one of my old work colleagues, they’d contacted me and we were talking about old memories and stuff. We went through and he just said it’s weird, you’re doing all these videos because you’ve never done anything like this. And I said, yeah, but I kind of got really into conferences towards the end of my career and I wasn’t really into them at the beginning — beginning, and we were talking about why that was and I don’t know if it’s different, if it’s a difference in me or a difference in the conferences — but it kind of made me go back and think: um, I’ve only really worked at five companies my whole life, at least as an employee. So that was, you know, uh, Pegasus, which changed its name to cyberon, interplay, Troika, carbine and obsidian, now Pegasus, cyberon.

item.title: GDC Showcase 2023
item.description: Hey everyone. I just wanted to announce that on Wednesday, June 28th at 1 pm, the GDC showcase is going to re-air my classic game postmortem on Fallout, and I will be doing a live chat at the same time, answering all the attendees Fallout related questions. There’s a link below, if you want to attend, thanks.
